message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_22-41-54_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix _opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "cursed sword item modifier adds 50% surcharge to that item's base"
Expected: cursed sword (newcomer, ench 3) → premium 165 G
Current error: Expected 165, received 115
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_22-41-54_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Add curse surcharge: when item.cursed === true, add 50% of item base to item subtotal.

The premium formula needs to think about:
- Each item contributes: base_for_item + (curse surcharge: 0.5 × base if cursed)
- First insurance: 10% × sum of all item bases (still based on raw base premium per spec)
- Fee: 5

For cursed sword: 100 (sword base) + 50 (curse) = 150 item subtotal. First = 10% of 100 = 10. Policy subtotal = 150 + 10 + 5 = 165.

Update the quote function. Keep existing tests green. For now, only swords can be cursed (BASE_PREMIUMS items, not components). Components don't have curse/enchantment.

Run pnpm test to verify all 13 tests pass.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal change needed.
Now I'll implement the minimal change to add the curse surcharge.

content.text:
All 13 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added optional `cursed` field to item type, and tracked `itemSubtotal` separately from `base`. When an item has `cursed === true`, adds 50% of its base to the item subtotal. The first insurance rate (10%) is still computed against the raw `base` (sum of unmodified item bases) per the spec.
**Result**: All 13 tests now pass (37 todo remaining)
**Approach**: Minimal change - introduced one new accumulator (`itemSubtotal`) and a single conditional for the curse surcharge. Kept components flowing through unchanged since they don't have curse/enchantment. The premium formula now uses `itemSubtotal` (which equals `base` when no items are cursed, preserving all previously-passing tests) plus first insurance computed on raw `base` plus the processing fee.

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_22-41-54_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
